turns.name: CHIEF JUSTICE ROBERTS
turns.text: In Case 23-14, Diaz versus United States, Justice Thomas has the opinion of the Court.

turns.name: JUSTICE THOMAS
turns.text: This case comes to us on a writ of certiorari to the United States Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit. Delilah Diaz was stopped at a port of entry while trying to drive across the US-Mexico border. Border patrols searched Diaz’s car and found more than 54 pound of methamphetamine hidden in the car’s door panels. Diaz was charged with knowingly importing methamphetamine. In her defense, Diaz claimed that she did not know that there were drugs in the car and suggested that her boyfriend secretly put the drugs there. To rebut Diaz’s defense, the government called Special Agent Andrew Flood as an expert witness. He testified that most couriers know that they are transporting drugs. The jury found Diaz guilty. Diaz appealed and challenged Agent Flood’s testimony under Federal Rule of Evidence 704(b). That Rule states that "In a criminal case, an expert witness must not state an opinion about whether the defendant did or did not have a mental state or condition that constitutes an element of the crime charged or of a defense." The Court of Appeals affirmed Diaz’s conviction. In an opinion filed with the Clerk today, we affirm. Rule 704(b) applies only to opinions about the defendants. Agent Flood however did not express an opinion about Diaz. Instead, he testified about the knowledge of most drug couriers. That opinion does not necessarily describe Diaz’s mental state. In fact, Diaz may or may not have been like most drug couriers. Diaz herself made this point at trial by arguing that her boyfriend planted the drugs found in the car. The ultimate issue of Diaz’s mental state was left to the jury. Agent Flood’s testimony thus did not violate Rule 704(b). For this reason and other set forth in our opinion, we affirm the judgment of the Ninth Circuit and remand for further proceedings consistent with this opinion. Justice Jackson has filed a concurring opinion. Justice Gorsuch has filed a dissenting opinion, in which Justices Sotomayor and Kagan join.
